title: Writing - Part 1: Food Waste
description: Your class has discussed the growing problem of food waste and the measures that could help reduce it.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the measures in your notes. You should explain which measure you consider most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: People throw away food because they do not understand how to store it properly or plan their meals.
Supermarkets encourage overbuying with multi-buy offers and large packaging.
Without legal obligations to reduce waste, businesses have little incentive to change.

structure.intro: Introduce the food waste problem and the two measures you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first measure and why it could be effective.
structure.body2: Discuss the second measure and compare it to the first.
structure.conclusion: State your opinion on which measure should be prioritised.

content.modelAnswer:
Food waste is a significant global problem. Every year, millions of tonnes of edible food are discarded while many people go hungry. Two measures that could help tackle this issue are consumer education and stricter supermarket policies.

Educating consumers about meal planning, food storage and portion sizes could prevent a great deal of unnecessary waste. Many households throw away food simply because they buy more than they need or do not know how to make the most of leftovers. Schools and public information campaigns could play a vital role in changing these habits over time.

Supermarkets, however, are responsible for encouraging excessive purchasing through special offers and oversized portions. If stores were required to sell more flexible quantities and donate unsold food to charities rather than disposing of it, waste could be reduced substantially at the source.

In my view, changing supermarket policies is the more important measure. While consumer awareness is valuable, individual habits are heavily influenced by what shops make available and attractive. Addressing the problem at the retail level would have a broader and more immediate impact on reducing food waste across society.
